rulings.Case Title: PAUL YETT VS. MARK KATZMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 21, 2011, Line 5, DEFENDANTs PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC., DEBORAH LOPEZ's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion is denied as a matter of law. The administrative decision by the Residential Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Board did not actually litigate Ms. Lopez's intent. Ms. Lopez was not a party to the administrative proceeding. Her intent was not germane to resolving the issue before the hearing officer, i.e., whether the construction was in fact new and exempt under section 37.2(r)(5) of the San Francisco Administrative Code. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BY AND THROUGH VS. NEXT SEAFOOD COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion On Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 21, 2011, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion On Judgment On The Pleadings. The Courts grants and denies judgment on the pleadings on the answer and cross- complaint as follows: The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the first affirmative defense, the first and second causes of action for breach of contract, and third cause of action for promissory estoppel. Those defenses and claims are based on the 10-year lease, and the unlawful detainer judgment adjudicated that the parties had a month-to-month tenancy. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the second defense for constitutional violations (contract clause and privileges and immunities). Those defenses are inapposite and defendant does not argue otherwise in opposition to the motion. The Court denies as to third defense (Burton Act), fourth defense (discrimination), and fourth cause of action for civil rights violations. The record is unclear whether the issues regarding the Burton Act and the alleged discrimination were actually litigated in the unlawful detainer given that Judge Busch struck those issues from his order. See Vella v. Hudgins, 20 Cal. 3d 251, 258 (1977) ("We decline to assume, given the summary character of this type of action, that the mere pleading of a defense without objection by the adverse party necessarily demonstrates adequate opportunity to litigate the defense.") Defendant may have leave to amend to plead a conversion cause of action. In the Court's May 16, 2011 ruling regarding the equipment, it expressly refused to determine whether the equipment reverted to the landlord. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM SHRYOCK et al VS. KELLI GAZICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 21, 2011, Line 17, PLAINTIFF ADAM SHRYOCK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained, with ten days leave to amend, as to the third, fifth, sixth and seventh causes of action. Overruled as to the first, second, fourth, eighth, ninth and tenth causes of action as Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts. For the third cause of action, Cross-Complainant must allege whether the contract was oral, written or implied by conduct. For the fifth cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not alleged that Cross-Defendants' conduct was outrageous or a substantial factor in causing her severe emotional distress. For the sixth cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not alleged that the statements made were false. For the seventh cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not pled a legally protected privacy interest, a reasonable expectation of privacy in the circumstances, and conduct by Cross-Defendants constituted a serious invasion of privacy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH COOPER et al VS. KAGAN CERAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Motion To Compel Arb And To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, For Stay Pending Arb; Request Forjudicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 21, 2011, Line 19, DEFENDANT KAGAN CERAN, EMI CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, For Stay Pending Arbitration; Request For Judicial Notice. The motion is continued to January 12, 2012. The opposing party shall lodge a courtesy copy of the opposition with department 302 per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Please attach a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
